1. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/resolve_objective_ref.py` 2. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_apply_landed_update.py` 3. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_fetch_context.py` 4. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/plan_save.py`

Read each file first, then apply all renames using the Edit tool.

session.tool_result:
1→"""Resolve an objective reference to an objective number. 2→ 3→Consolidates argument parsing + branch inference for objective-plan command. 4→ 5→Resolution cascade: 6→1. Non-empty ref → parse as number/URL 7→2. Empty ref + branch matches objective pattern → extract from branch name 8→3. Empty ref + plan metadata lookup → get objective_id from branch's plan 9→4. All fail → {"resolved": false} 10→ 11→Usage: 12→ erk exec resolve-objective-ref 13→ erk exec resolve-objective-ref 3679 14→ erk exec resolve-objective-ref https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/3679 15→ 16→Output: 17→ JSON with resolution result: 18→ {"resolved": true, "objective_number": 3679, "source": "argument"} 19→ {"resolved": true, "objective_number": 456, "source": "branch_name"} 20→ {"resolved": true, "objective_number": 123, "source": "plan_metadata"} 21→ {"resolved": false} 22→ 23→Exit Codes: 24→ 0: Always (caller decides how to handle not-resolved) 25→""" 26→ 27→import json 28→from collections.abc import Callable 29→ 30→import click
... [99 lines omitted] ...
110→ except RuntimeError:

1→"""Apply mechanical updates to an objective after landing a PR. 2→ 3→Combines the fetch-context, update-nodes, and post-action-comment steps into 4→a single call, eliminating 5+ sequential agent commands. Only prose 5→reconciliation (which requires LLM judgment) is left to the calling skill. 6→ 7→The PR number doubles as the plan number (in erk, the PR IS the plan). 8→ 9→Usage: 10→ erk exec objective-apply-landed-update --pr 6517 --objective 6423 --branch P6517-... 11→ erk exec objective-apply-landed-update # auto-discovers all arguments 12→ 13→Output: 14→ JSON with {success, objective, plan, pr, roadmap, node_updates, 15→ action_comment_id} or {success, error} 16→ 17→Exit Codes: 18→ 0: Success - all mechanical updates applied 19→ 1: Error - missing data or API failure 20→""" 21→ 22→import json 23→ 24→import click 25→ 26→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.objective_fetch_context import ( 27→ _build_roadmap_context, 28→ _fetch_objective_content, 29→) 30→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.objective_post_action_comment import (
... [320 lines omitted] ...
57→ ApplyLandedUpdateErrorDict, 69→def _error_json(error: str) -> str: 70→ result: ApplyLandedUpdateErrorDict = {"success": False, "error": error} 194→ click.echo(_error_json("Cannot determine owner/repo from current context")) 206→ click.echo(_error_json("Could not determine current branch (detached HEAD?)")) 213→ click.echo(_error_json(f"No PR found for branch '{branch_name}'")) 220→ click.echo(_error_json(f"PR #{pr_number} not found")) 229→ click.echo(_error_json(msg)) 236→ click.echo(_error_json(f"Objective #{objective_number} not found")) 242→ click.echo(_error_json(f"PR #{pr_number} not found"))

1→"""Fetch all context needed for objective-update-with-landed-pr in one call. 2→ 3→Bundles objective, plan, PR details, and parsed roadmap context 4→into a single JSON blob, eliminating multiple sequential LLM turns for data 5→fetching and step matching. 6→ 7→Usage: 8→ erk exec objective-fetch-context --pr 6517 --objective 6423 --branch plnd/... 9→ erk exec objective-fetch-context # auto-discovers all arguments 10→ 11→Output: 12→ JSON with {success, objective, plan, pr, roadmap} or {success, error} 13→ 14→Exit Codes: 15→ 0: Success - all data fetched 16→ 1: Error - missing data or invalid arguments 17→""" 18→ 19→import json 20→ 21→import click 22→ 23→from erk.cli.repo_resolution import get_remote_github 24→from erk_shared.context.helpers import ( 25→ require_context, 26→ require_cwd, 27→ require_git, 28→ require_github, 29→ require_plan_backend, 30→ require_repo_root,

53→ ObjectiveFetchContextErrorDict, 63→def _error_json(error: str) -> str: 64→ result: ObjectiveFetchContextErrorDict = {"success": False, "error": error} 176→ click.echo(_error_json("Cannot determine owner/repo from current context")) 186→ click.echo(_error_json(f"PR #{pr_number_arg} not found")) 196→ click.echo(_error_json("Could not determine current branch (detached HEAD?)")) 202→ click.echo(_error_json(f"No PR found for branch '{branch_name}'")) 210→ click.echo(_error_json(msg)) 217→ click.echo(_error_json("Cannot auto-discover PR without --branch or --pr")) 221→ click.echo(_error_json(f"No PR found for branch '{branch_name}'")) 228→ click.echo(_error_json(f"Objective issue #{objective_number} not found")) 234→ click.echo(_error_json(f"PR #{pr_number} not found"))

1→"""Plan save: creates a planned PR with the plan content. 2→ 3→Usage: 4→ erk exec plan-save [OPTIONS] 5→ 6→Options: 7→ --format json|display: Output format (default: json) 8→ --plan-file PATH: Use specific plan file (highest priority) 9→ --session-id ID: Session ID for scoped plan lookup 10→ --plan-type standard|learn: Plan type (default: standard) 11→ --learned-from-issue INT: Parent plan (for learn plans) 12→ --created-from-workflow-run-url URL: Workflow run URL 13→ 14→Objective linking: use --objective to pass directly, or rely on 15→automatic linking via the objective-context session marker 16→(created by /erk:objective-plan). The --objective flag takes 17→precedence over the session marker. 18→ 19→Exit Codes: 20→ 0: Success 21→ 1: Error 22→ 2: Validation failed 23→""" 24→ 25→import json 26→from pathlib import Path 27→ 28→import click 29→ 30→from erk.cli.commands.exec.scripts.validate_plan_content import _validate_plan_content
... [560 lines omitted] ...

Excellent! Now let me create a summary of the changes made:
## Summary
I have successfully completed the renaming task across all four files. Here's what was changed:
### Files Modified
1. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/resolve_objective_ref.py`** - Line 35: `require_plan_backend` → `require_pr_backend` (import) - Line 103: `plan_backend` → `pr_backend` (variable assignment) - Line 109: `plan_backend.get_managed_pr_for_branch()` → `pr_backend.get_managed_pr_for_branch()` (method call)
2. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_apply_landed_update.py`** - Line 43: `require_plan_backend` → `require_pr_backend` (import) - Line 189: `plan_backend` → `pr_backend` (variable assignment) - Line 218: `plan_backend.get_managed_pr()` → `pr_backend.get_managed_pr()` (method call)
3. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_fetch_context.py`** - Line 29: `require_plan_backend` → `require_pr_backend` (import) - Line 172: `plan_backend` → `pr_backend` (variable assignment) - Line 184: `plan_backend.get_managed_pr()` → `pr_backend.get_managed_pr()` (method call) - Line 200: `plan_backend.get_managed_pr_for_branch()` → `pr_backend.get_managed_pr_for_branch()` (method call)
4. **`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/plan_save.py`** - No changes needed. The only occurrences of `plan_backend` in this file are external API field names (`"plan_backend": "planned_pr"` on lines 355 and 446), which were correctly excluded from renaming per your instructions.
All renames have been applied to imports, parameter names, variable names, and attribute accesses. No external API field names or user-facing messages were modified.
